title: Rf class ab cascode amplifier with linearization and steering diodes
abstract: Systems and methods for amplifying signals. In some embodiments, the signals may be amplified using a diode steering network with an amplifier operated in class AB mode. In some embodiments, distortion in the amplified signal may be corrected using a feed forward cancellation circuit operated in class A mode.
claims:
Claims 1. A class AB amplifier circuit comprising: an input capable of receiving an input signal that drives first and second transistors arranged in a differential pair, the transistors amplifying the input signal; an output for the amplified input signal from the first and second transistors; the output comprising a first load and a second load a steering network comprising a plurality of steering diodes, the steering network selectively directing a first phase of the input signal to the first load and a second phase of the input signal to the second load. 2. The amplifier circuit of claim 1 including a cascode output stage receiving the amplified output signal from the first load and the second load. 3. The amplifier circuit of claim 2 including feed-forward distortion cancellation. 4. The amplifier circuit of claim 2 including a third transistor and a fourth transistor provided with the input signal and a signal representative of the amplifier output current so as to create an error signal used to cancel distortion in the output of the amplifier circuit. 5. The amplifier circuit of claim 4 where the third transistor and the fourth transistor operate in class A mode. 6. The amplifier circuit of claim 2 including a variable power supply. 7. The amplifier circuit of claim 6 where the dynamic power supply is modulated using a signal envelope based on the input signal. 8. The amplifier circuit of claim 2 where an output transformer includes a plurality of windings that produce a feedback signal used in a feedback distortion cancellation circuit. 9. A feed forward distortion cancellation circuit comprising: 

 a first pair of transistors amplifying an input signal; and a second pair of transistors arranged in a cascade output stage, the second pair of transistors substantially reproduce the currents in the collectors of the first pair of transistors. 10. The feed forward cancellation circuit of claim 9 where the base of each of the second pair of transistors receive a copy of the input signal. 11. The feed forward cancellation circuit of claim 10 where the second pair of transistors outputs an error signal used to cancel distortion in the amplified output of the first pair of transistors. 12. The feed forward cancellation circuit of claim 9 including feedback distortion cancellation. 13. The feed forward cancellation circuit of claim 12 where the second pair of transistors operate in class A mode. 14. The feed forward cancellation circuit of claim 13 where the second pair of transistors are used to provide feed forward cancellation. 15. A method for correcting distortion in an amplified input signal the method comprising: receiving an input signal; generating an amplified signal in a first stage using the input signal, the first stage operated in class AB mode generating an error signal in a second stage using the input signal, the second stage operated in class A mode; and using the error signal to correct distortion in the amplified signal. 16. The method of claim 15 including the step of steering a first phase of the input signal to a first transistor and steering a second phase of the input signal to a second transistor. 17. The method of claim 16 including the step of modulating a voltage supply using the steering network. 18. The method of claim 17 where the modulated voltage supply drives an RF amplifier. 19. The method of claim 18 where the modulated supply voltage is superimposed on a DC voltage. 20. The method of claim 16 including the step of reducing distortion in the amplified signal using feedback.
